title: Human endometrium and endometriosis tissue dissociation for single-cell RNA sequencing
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7pa1b","text":"Tissue dissociation is critical step to ensure the quality of samples for single-cell RNA sequencing. To ensure recovery of most cells in the tissue without introducing stress response from the dissociation process, we adapted the dissociation method by Adam et al. (2017) and optimized for the dissociation of human endometrium and endometriosis tissue (both ectopic peritoneal and ovary lesions), targeting high quality viable cells (above 70%) with high transcript and gene counts per cell.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":259,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"6rq28","text":"This protocol was adapted from Adam et al. (2017) and optimized for dissociation of human endometrium and endometriosis tissue (from ectopic peritoneal and ovary lesion) for single-cell RNA sequencing, yielding high UMI and gene counts per cell.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":36,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e6bj4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"85dmb","text":"Tissue was collected fresh from the operating room and immediately submerged in MACS Storage Buffer before transport to laboratory on ice. Processing of tissue shortly after receiving will ensure high cell viability and (hopefully) high quality single-cell RNA sequencing data.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1ic90","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"ctt5r","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"citation","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"guid":"4961B499341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","title":"Psychrophilic proteases dramatically reduce single-cell RNA-seq artifacts: a molecular atlas of kidney development.","protocol_guid":"","journal":"Development (Cambridge, England)","date":"2017-10-01T00:00:00Z","created_on":"2026-02-10T22:33:37Z","doi":"https://doi.org/10.1242/dev.151142","authors":"Adam M, Potter AS, Potter SS","can_edit":false}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"asms3","text":"Coat all tubes and tips with serum buffer, e.g. 2.5% BSA or \"Buffer I\" used in this protocol","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"988e","text":"Ensure centrifuge is set to 4°C.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"smbh","text":"Prepare cold water bath (6°C). ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7rmf4","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"b16hl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cgtak","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"rcq8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7kb2f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"notes","mutability":"","data":{"blocks":[{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[{"length":10,"offset":0,"style":"bold"}],"key":"2umj1","text":"IMPORTANT: ","type":"unstyled"},{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"ercc6","text":"Keep the cells cold (on ice) at all times, except when specifically mentioned in the protocol.","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}}},"1":{"type":"notes","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"blocks":[{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"eamd0","text":"Alternative to cold water bath: ","type":"unstyled"},{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"ejeq5","text":"Fill ice bucket up to 3/4 of the volume, then top off with distilled water. ","type":"ordered-list-item"},{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"bo20l","text":"Let the ice sit on bench for few minutes and measure the temperature.","type":"ordered-list-item"}],"entityMap":{}}}}}

steps: Prepare fresh digestion enzyme - Cold Active Protease (CAP) solution: Reagents Stock conc. Final conc. Vol. 1 Cold Active Protease 100 mg/mL 10 mg/mL 0.2 mL 2 DPBS 1X 1X 1.8 mL 3 CaCl 1M 5 mM 60 uL 4 DNAseI 1mg/mL 8.3 uL Abbreviation: conc. = concentration vol = volume

Stain cells with viability dye ( Propidium Iodide and Calcein Violet ) following manufacturer's protocol, incubate 00:20:00, on ice . Propidium iodide staining solution Becton Dickinson (BD) Catalog #556463 CellTrace™ Calcein Violet, AM, for 405 nm excitation - Special Packaging Thermo Fisher Catalog #C34858
Mince the tissue to 1 mm 2 cube with sterile scalpel.
Add 2 mL, CAP solution into the dish.

Transfer all materials from the dish to GentleMACS C Tubes using wide-bore tips, then incubate in cold water bath for 7-10 minutes. (Coat the gentleMACS C-Tube Miltenyi Biotec Catalog #130-093-334 with 2.5 % (v/v), BSA solution before use)

Place tube on gentleMACS Dissociator and run “ mouse_brain_3 ” protocol. Repeat twice (2x). gentleMACS (gentleMACS™ Octo Dissociator with Heaters, Miltenyi Biotec, 130-096-427, https://www.miltenyibiotec.com/CH-en/products/gentlemacs-octo-dissociator-with-heaters.html#130-096-427 )
When the run is finished, place the tube in cold water bath and let tissue settle down for 00:00:30, to 00:01:00

Transfer 1-1.5 mL supernatant containing dissociated single-cells to 50 mL conical tube containing 30 mL, Buffer I . Do not disturb the non-dissociated tissue fragments at the base of the tube. CRITICAL STEP: Keep collected cell suspension on ice until the tissue dissociation procedure is completed.

Triturate the mixture with 1000 µL wide-bore tips for 00:00:15 every 00:02:00 to ensure homogenization and dissociation. We recommend the use of 1000 µL wide-bore tips for large tissue chucks and ensuring good homogenization and dissociation. However, most eutopic endometrium consists of mucuos layer and may be better dissociated with standard 1000 µL tips.
Add another freshly made 2 mL, CAP solution into gentleMACS C Tubes containing the remaining non-dissociated tissue fragments. Incubate in cold water bath for 00:20:00, to 00:40:00 .
Triturate the mixture with 1000 µL wide-bore tips for 00:00:15 every 00:05:00, incubation to ensure homogenization and dissociation.

Place tube on gentleMACS Dissociator and run “ mouse_brain_3 ” protocol every 00:15:00, incubation gentleMACS™ Dissociator (tissue dissociator, Miltenyi Biotec, 130-093-235, 8 tubes, etc.., https://www.miltenyibiotec.com/US-en/products/gentlemacs-dissociator.html )

Load 12,000 cells using the Chromium Next GEM Single Cell 3-prime kit v3.1, onto a 10x Chip G and process according to manufacturer's protocol (CG000315 Rev A). Chromium Controller (microfuidic based single-cell capture, 10x Genomics, 1000171, https://www.10xgenomics.com/instruments/chromium-controller ) (cat#) onto 10x Chip (cat#), rocess according to manufacturer's protocol. Chromium Next GEM Single Cell 3-prime Kit v3.1 10x Genomics Catalog #1000268 Chromium Next GEM Chip G Kit 10x Genomics Catalog #1000120 cells using the Chromium Next GEM Single Cell 3-prime kit v3.1, onto a 10x Chip G and process according to manufacturer's protocol (CG000315 Rev A.
Repeat go to step #11 . Transfer the supernatant to the 50 ml conical tube containing single cells from step 8 .

Observe the leftover tissue fragments under microscope. Leftover tissue fragments may contain fibers (extracellular matrix) and/or cells. If there are few or no cells attached, go to step # . otherwise, follow the step below. Do this step only if tissue is not completely dissociated.
Triturate with 1000 µL wide-bore tips to release leftover cells.

Pulse centrifuge ( 520 x g, 4 °C, , 10 - 15 seconds ) to pellet tissue fragments, then collect as much supernatant as possible into the tube containing single cells from step 11. Do not disturb the non-dissociated tissue fragments at the base of the tube.
Centrifuge the collected cell suspension 800 x g, 4 °C, 8 minutes , then discard the supernatant .
Resuspend the cell pellet with 2 mL, ACK Lysing buffer , incubate on ice for 2-5 minutes to remove erythrocytes. ACK Lysing Buffer Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #A1049201 .
Add 45 mL, Buffer I to stop ACK Lysis buffer reaction, then collect cells by centrifugation 800 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes .

Resuspend the cell pellet in 2 mL, Buffer II or Cell Staining Buffer BioLegend Catalog #420201 Observe the cell suspension under the microscope. If aggregates of cells are observed, pellet the cells, remove the supernatant and continue dissociation with 500 µL, TrypLE Express Enzyme for 00:02:00, to 00:03:00 , at 37 °C . Add 4.5 mL, Buffer II to stop the reaction, pellet the cells and resuspend in either Buffer II or Cell Staining Buffer . TrypLE™ Express Enzyme Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #12604013

Filter the cell suspension through MACS SmartStrainers (70 µm) Miltenyi Biotec Catalog #130-098-462 into Protein LoBind 5 mL Tube Fisher Scientific Catalog #14-282-304 to remove debris and non-dissociated tissue fragments. The cell suspension is ready for flow cytometry cell staining and sorting. Note: We recommend filtering the cells before sorting through a Falcon 5 mL Polystyrene Round-Bottom Tube with Cell-Strainer Cap Fisher Scientific Catalog #352235

Sort viable cells (PI-/Calcein Violet+) onto 5 mL collection tube (LoBind Eppendorf 5ml tube) containing 300 µL, Buffer II . We target to collect at least 40,000 cells per sample. FACSAria Fusion (Flow cytometer, BD, Special order)
Top up the tube with 0.04 % (v/v), BSA solution up to 5 mL , then centrifuge 2000 rpm, 4 °C, 5 minutes to collect cells. Remove supernatant carefully until left with around 30-50 µL, of supernatant .

Resuspend carefully with 200 µL tips. Count the number of cells and cell viability with Countess FLII or similar preferred cell counting methodology. Countess II (AMQAX1000, Life Technologies/Invitrogen, None, https://www.thermofisher.com/search/results?query=countess+ii&navId=26204&persona=Catalog , https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/public/c0ffc7e38c31955ccd359f4beb71fd1021987d95522892c81fd294e137bd44e3/csi8bghg7.webp , https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/public/c0ffc7e38c31955ccd359f4beb71fd1021987d95522892c81fd294e137bd44e3/csi9bghg7.pdf )
Place 60 mm 2 dish On ice , then use sterile forceps to transfer tissue from MACS Tissue Storage Solution Miltenyi Biotec Catalog #130-100-008 onto the dish.
Add 3 mL, Dispase solution into gentleMACS C tube. Placed the tube on gentleMACS Dissociator and Run “ 37C_h_TDK3 ” protocol for 00:15:00 . This protocol requires the temperature set to 37 °C .

Leftover single-cells from this protocol can be used to generate endometrium organoids. To generate organoids, we utilize the protocol made by Sheridan et al. (2020) grown in endometrium organoid culture media optimized by Boretto et al. (2019). Establishment and differentiation of long-term trophoblast organoid cultures from the human placenta.. Nature protocols. Patient-derived organoids from endometrial disease capture clinical heterogeneity and are amenable to drug screening.. Nature cell biology.
Prepare fresh digestion enzyme - Dispase solution: add 500 µL, Dispase (100 mg/mL) add 10 mL, DPBS (1X) add 40 µL, DNAseI (1 mg/mL)
Prepare Buffer I (For Washing and Collection of Dissociated Cells) DPBS 2 mM EDTA 2% BSA 15% FBS
Prepare Buffer II (For Collection of Sorted Cells) Advanced DMEM/F12 (1X) 1X Glutamax 10 mM HEPES 10% FBS 1% BSA
Prepare Coating Buffer (For coating tubes/tips) 2.5% BSA (keep On ice ).
